_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning how to work well with others in a group and make sure everyone gets a fair turn to contribute.

_source.w: We are learning how to work well with others in a group and make sure everyone gets a fair turn to contribute.
y: Working together helps us achieve things we couldn't do alone.
Learning to collaborate now prepares us for teamwork in sports, projects, and future jobs.
Checking that everyone contributes makes the group fairer and helps everyone feel valued.
eg: We might take turns being the leader, the recorder, and the presenter in a group project.
We could practise sharing materials and waiting for our turn to use something.
We might check in with our group by asking 'Does everyone agree?' or 'What do you think?'
We could talk about what to do if one person is doing all the work and others aren't helping.
We might give each group member a specific job so everyone knows what they need to do.
We could reflect after a group task on what went well and what we could do better next time.
